appliedUpdates.description: Fixed architecture.md: changed 'requireScope()' to 'enforceScope()' to match the actual function name exported from permissions.ts
Added 'MCP Server Patterns' section to architecture.md documenting InMemoryTransport testing pattern, stdio safety, auth resolution, and prompt argument constraints

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: All routes using getResolvedContext() must also call enforceScope(ctx, 'required:scope') after auth resolution. Currently /api/health-data is missing enforceScope(ctx, 'health:read') while sibling /series and /periods routes enforce it, creating an inconsistent scope enforcement surface.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: apps/web/src/app/api/health-data/route.ts has no enforceScope call; apps/web/src/app/api/health-data/series/route.ts and periods/route.ts both call enforceScope(ctx, 'health:read'). An API key without health:read scope can still access daily health data at the main endpoint.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: backend-worker skill: Add note that test-first (red phase) is flexible for MCP/integration-heavy features where the server structure must exist before transport tests can connect. Worker deviated correctly by implementing and testing in parallel.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-server worker noted in skillFeedback that test-first is impractical for MCP server work because InMemoryTransport tests need the McpServer instance to exist. Parallel implementation+testing was the pragmatic approach.

rejectedObservations.observation: stdio convention reinforcement (from mcp-server review): architecture.md CLI Patterns section could reinforce the no-console.log rule
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in AGENTS.md coding conventions ('Never use console.log() in MCP server code') and now also in the new MCP Server Patterns section of architecture.md. No additional reinforcement needed.
